name: Angels and Airwaves
bio: Tom DeLonge's epic cinematic alternative rock project blending massive atmospheric guitars, spacey synth layers, and themes of hope, dreams, interstellar travel, and human connection. Signature sound: reverb-drenched walls of sound that feel like launching into the cosmos.
producer_notes: Primarily self-produced by Tom DeLonge with Roland Fantom X6 synths for ethereal pads, Gibson ES-335 and Fender guitars through Vox AC30/Mesa Boogie amps, heavy delay/reverb pedals, live pulsing drums, and Tom Lord-Alge mixing for compressed epic vocals and wide cinematic stereo field.
style_prompt: Cinematic atmospheric alternative rock with massive reverb-drenched electric guitars and long delay tails creating infinite space, layered ethereal synth pads and arpeggios, pulsing live drums with epic fills and builds, earnest male tenor vocals processed with heavy plate reverb and echo for vast interstellar feel, dynamic quiet-loud-louder arcs, polished cinematic production with orchestral swells and wide stereo field, hopeful rebellious uplifting energy that feels like staring at the stars or launching into the unknown, mid-tempo to up-tempo grooves with dramatic swells
lyric_prompt: Write a cinematic atmospheric alternative rock song about humanity's longing for connection, hope amid darkness, space travel, dreams of a better world, or interstellar love and rebellion. Perspective: a dreamer or visionary looking to the stars. Tone: earnest and uplifting yet melancholic with a sense of wonder and urgency. Emotional core: we are not alone — the adventure awaits, love conquers distance. Chorus is a massive soaring singalong release with echoed gang layers. Include cosmic imagery: stars, moons, rockets, night skies, distant horizons, floating through space. Structure: atmospheric verse with building tension, explosive hopeful chorus, reflective bridge, final massive chorus. Vary the exact story, narrator's journey, specific metaphors, relationship details, and outcome while staying in this exact emotional and sonic world. Allow full creative freedom for original narratives and cosmic metaphors. Aim for 4:00-6:00 runtime with epic builds.
recommended_mixes: Pair with Blink-182 for emotional contrast or The Killers for synth-rock energy

songs.title: The Adventure
songs.dna: Reverb-drenched Gibson guitars, Roland Fantom synth layers, Tom DeLonge self-production with massive delay tails and cinematic builds.
songs.style_prompt: Cinematic atmospheric alternative rock with massive reverb-drenched guitars, ethereal synth pads, pulsing drums, earnest male tenor with heavy echo, hopeful interstellar energy
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a cinematic alternative rock song about launching into the unknown and finding hope in the stars. Tone: earnest wonder. Vary the journey while keeping the adventure emotional core. Under 5:00.
songs.variation_guidance: Vary the destination or narrator's fear while keeping the hopeful launch core
songs.hook_focus: Soaring title chorus with echoed layers

songs.title: A Little's Enough
songs.dna: Acoustic-driven with synth swells and heavy reverb.
songs.style_prompt: Cinematic atmospheric alternative rock with reverb guitars, synth layers, earnest tenor, uplifting hopeful energy
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a cinematic alternative rock song about how a little hope or love is enough to change everything. Tone: sincere wonder. Vary the small moment while keeping the enough emotional core.
songs.variation_guidance: Vary the specific 'little' thing while keeping hope core

songs.lyric_prompt: Write a cinematic alternative rock song about everything feeling magical when you're with the right person or in the right moment. Tone: playful wonder. Vary the magic source while keeping the everything's magic core.
songs.variation_guidance: Vary the everyday scene while keeping magical core

songs.variation_guidance: Vary the rebellion while keeping hopeful core

songs.lyric_prompt: Write a cinematic alternative rock song about everything we are together in love or humanity. Tone: heartfelt connection. Vary the 'we' story while keeping the all-that-we-are core.
songs.variation_guidance: Vary the relationship or collective while keeping connection core
